name: Confluence
tagline: Team collaboration and knowledge management hub
description: Create, share, and organize team knowledge with connected documentation
pros: Deep Jira integration linking documentation to developer workflows
Whiteboards with action items converting into Jira tickets
300+ templates for engineering, product, and business documentation
Spaces with granular permissions for org-wide knowledge management
Confluence AI summarizing pages and suggesting related content
cons: Search effectiveness declines significantly across 5,000+ pages
Page editor feels dated versus Notion's block-based system
Self-hosted Data Center pricing exceeds $40K/year for 500 users

content.title: Executive Summary
content.body: Create, share, and organize team knowledge with connected documentation Confluence earns a 4.1/5 rating from 32,400 user reviews.

content.title: TL;DR
content.body: Confluence is a leading project management platform rated 4.1/5 across 32,400 reviews. Pricing starts at Free–$11/user/mo. Best for Engineering Teams and Product Teams.

content.title: Rating Overview
content.body: Confluence scores 4.1/5 overall with ease of use (3.8/5), features (4.2/5), performance (4/5), support (3.7/5), value (4/5).

content.title: Quick Verdict
content.body: Confluence earns a 4.1/5 rating recommended for Engineering Teams and Product Teams. Delivers solid functionality with functional design.

content.title: Company Background
content.body: Confluence was founded in 2004 in San Francisco, USA, serving 100K+ teams customers with 5,000+ employees.

content.title: Product Overview
content.body: Confluence is a team collaboration and knowledge management hub. Provides 18 features across Core, Analytics, Security, AI, Integrations, Advanced categories. Self-hosted available. Mobile on iOS and Android. API available.

content.title: Key Features
content.body: Essential project management features:
items: Pages: Rich text with inline comments.
Whiteboards: Visual collaboration with Jira sync.

content.title: Feature Deep Dive
content.body: Pages. Rich text with inline comments. Spaces. Organized content workspaces. Whiteboards. Visual collaboration with Jira sync.

content.title: AI Features
content.body: Confluence AI includes AI content suggestions, Smart search, AI page summaries.

content.title: API & Automation
content.body: Confluence provides a comprehensive API for custom integration and workflow automation.

content.title: Security
content.body: Confluence holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP certifications with TLS 1.2+ and AES-256 encryption.

content.title: Compliance
content.body: Confluence complies with GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, SOC 2 regulations.

content.title: Integrations
content.body: Integrates with Jira, Slack, Trello, GitHub, Google Drive, Microsoft Teams, Figjam, Draw.io, and 2+ more.

content.title: Pricing
content.body: Confluence uses freemium with per-user premium. Free tier available. Plans from Free–$11/user/mo.

content.title: Pros
content.body: Confluence advantages:
items: Deep Jira integration linking documentation to developer workflows
Whiteboards with action items converting into Jira tickets
300+ templates for engineering, product, and business documentation
Spaces with granular permissions for org-wide knowledge management

content.title: Cons
content.body: Considerations:
items: Search effectiveness declines significantly across 5,000+ pages
Page editor feels dated versus Notion's block-based system
Self-hosted Data Center pricing exceeds $40K/year for 500 users

content.title: Performance
content.body: Adequate performance for most use cases.

content.title: Ease of Use
content.body: Moderate learning curve with comprehensive documentation.

content.title: User Experience
content.body: Functional organized interface.

content.title: Customer Support
content.body: Support rated 3.7/5 via email and knowledge base.

content.title: Industry Fit
content.body: Best for Knowledge Management and Collaboration with Engineering Teams and Product Teams teams.

content.title: Team Size Fit
content.body: Confluence accommodates teams of all sizes with generous free tier. Best ROI for 5-50 person teams.

content.title: Real Use Cases
content.body: A engineering teams team uses Confluence to streamline project management workflows and improve team productivity.

content.title: Competitor Analysis
content.body: Confluence competes with notion, monday-com, asana. Primary differentiators: core functionality and pricing.

content.title: Alternatives
content.body: Consider notion, monday-com, asana based on specific team requirements.

content.title: Migration Guide
content.body: Migration rated moderate complexity. Budget 2-4 weeks for full transition. Parallel testing recommended.

content.title: Buying Advice
content.body: Evaluate: feature alignment, total cost at Free–$11/user/mo, migration effort (2-4 weeks), and real-data trial.

content.title: Final Verdict
content.body: Confluence earns 4.1/5. Solid option for most teams. Recommended for teams prioritizing project management.

faqs.question: What is Confluence best used for?
faqs.answer: Confluence is best used for create, share, and organize team knowledge with connected documentation.

faqs.question: How much does Confluence cost?
faqs.answer: Confluence uses freemium with per-user premium starting at Free–$11/user/mo. Free tier available.

faqs.question: Does Confluence integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, integrates with Jira, Slack, Trello, GitHub, Google Drive. And 5+ more.

faqs.question: Is Confluence suitable for small teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a generous free tier. Freemium with per-user premium scales with team growth.

faqs.question: How does Confluence pricing work?
faqs.answer: Confluence uses freemium with per-user premium from Free–$11/user/mo, with free tier available.

faqs.question: Is Confluence secure?
faqs.answer: Yes, holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP certifications with encryption.

faqs.question: What integrations does Confluence offer?
faqs.answer: Integrates with Jira, Slack, Trello, GitHub, Google Drive, Microsoft Teams, and more. API available.

faqs.question: Is Confluence good for small businesses?
faqs.answer: Yes, with free tier. Freemium with per-user premium scales with business size.

faqs.question: What are Confluence's main advantages?
faqs.answer: Deep Jira integration linking documentation to developer wor and Whiteboards with action items converting into Jira tickets.

faqs.question: What are Confluence's limitations?
faqs.answer: Search effectiveness declines significantly across 5,000+ pages and Page editor feels dated versus Notion's block-based system.

faqs.question: How does Confluence compare to notion?
faqs.answer: Confluence differentiates through pricing and performance.

faqs.question: Does Confluence support team collaboration?
faqs.answer: Yes, includes collaboration features for team workflows.

faqs.question: Can I customize Confluence?
faqs.answer: Yes, via API, custom fields, and templates.

faqs.question: Is Confluence easy to set up?
faqs.answer: Setup takes a few days with documentation support.

faqs.question: How often does Confluence update?
faqs.answer: Updates monthly.

faqs.question: What support does Confluence provide?
faqs.answer: Support rated 3.7/5. Email and knowledge base.

faqs.question: Does Confluence offer a free version?
faqs.answer: Yes, with core features.

faqs.question: How does Confluence handle data privacy?
faqs.answer: Complies with GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, SOC 2 regulations.

faqs.question: What ROI can teams expect?
faqs.answer: Positive ROI typically within 3-6 months from improved project management workflows.

faqs.question: What platforms does Confluence support?
faqs.answer: Available on Cloud, Self-hosted. Mobile on iOS and Android.

author: StackPilot Team
learningCurve: Steep — requires dedicated training time for full proficiency
whoShouldUse: Confluence is ideal for small to mid-size teams that need a reliable project management solution without excessive complexity. It works well for organizations with 5-200 users who prioritize ease of use and quick deployment.
whoShouldNotUse: Confluence may be overwhelming for users who only need basic project management functionality. Its extensive feature set can create unnecessary complexity for simple use cases.
